rulings.Case Title: MICHAEL J PAROLI ET AL VS. SOPHIE LAU ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Bifurcate Trial
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Friday February 1, 2019 line 1. DEFENDANT SOPHIE LAU, LAUS INVESTMENT GROUP, JEFFREY LAU Notice Of Motion And Motion To Bifurcate Trial DENIED without prejudice to make the bifurcation/preclusion request before the trial judge. = (501/CFH)

rulings.Case Title: CHAU YU VS. TIGER WONG ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Cross Defendant Jeffrey Kwong'S Motion To Set Aside Default And Default Judgment, If Entered, And To Quash Service Of Summons
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Friday February 1, 2019 line 2. Cross Defendant Jeffrey Kwong'S Motion To Set Aside Default And Default Judgment, If Entered, And To Quash Service Of Summons DENIED. According to the Proof of Service by a registered process server on file, Mr. Kwong had been served on June 27, 2018. Motion fails to address this service and does not meet the burden under CCP 473(b) and CCP 473.5. = (501/CFH)

rulings.Case Title: JING ZHANG ET AL VS. TRINIDAD M. GWIN ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Notice Of Motion And Plaintiffs Motion For Summary Judgment Summary Adjudication Of Issues
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Friday February 1, 2019 line 5. PLAINTIFF JING ZHANG, JOHN GILLIN MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Notice Of Motion And Plaintiffs Motion For Summary Judgment Summary Adjudication Of Issues GRANTED in part and DENIED in part. Motion for Summary Judgment is DENIED. Triable issues of fact as to the affirmative defense of retaliation. As to the motion for Summary Adjudication the Court rules as follows: Issue One - DENIED. Plaintiff argues that these affirmative defenses as stated are an attack on Plaintiff's prima facie case and not a "new matter." While Plaintiff failed to analyze and argue why this position is correct as to each of the 13 affirmative defenses, those that are specified in the Points and Authorities appear to be more akin to denials than affirmative defenses. However, specific denials are appropriate in the answer and this motion does nothing to advance the case. In other words issues raised by Defendant's assertions in these affirmative defenses will be raised at trial whether or not they are labeled "affirmative defenses." Issue Two- DENIED. Triable issue of fact regarding retaliation/intent/motive. Issue Three - GRANTED. The burden of proof on these affirmative defenses is on defendant. Defendant failed to produce any admissible evidence that lender did not agree to the termination of tenancy (assuming that the tenant even has standing to assert this defense). Issue 4 - GRANTED in part and DENIED in part. Motion is DENIED as to 11th affirmative defense as it is based in part on retaliation. Motion is GRANTED as to defenses 20 and 31. The burden of proof on these affirmative defenses is on defendant. Defendant failed to produce any admissible evidence regarding discrimination at the time of the service of the Notice or the Unlawful Detainer Complaint. No authority is cited for the proposition that information regarding disability received 1.5 years after the initiation of Ellis Act can form the basis for the affirmative defense of discrimination. Issue 5 - GRANTED. There is no triable issue of fact regarding the parking. There is no evidence produced by the defendant that the terms of the agreement have changed. While the landlord might have disputed the tenant's right under the agreement to use the parking, the landlord affirmatively agreed to confer this benefit on the tenant, i.e. allow the parking. Issue 6 - DENIED. Moving party failed to cite any authority to supports its position and failed to point which evidence defendant is missing in order to prevail on these defenses. Issue 7 - GRANTED. No opposition filed. = (501/CFH)

rulings.Case Title: JING ZHANG ET AL VS. TRINIDAD M. GWIN ET AL
